sourceMovie.title: Ex Machina
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ex Machina works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Her
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Her draws from the same drama well as Ex Machina, channeling science-fiction in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2013 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Blade Runner 2049
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Blade Runner 2049 (2017) captures the drama appeal of Ex Machina without simply imitating it. The science-fiction streak running through both films is what puts Blade Runner 2049 at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: A.I. Artificial Intelligence
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama appeal of Ex Machina — that tense edge, the science-fiction — translates cleanly into A.I. Artificial Intelligence (2001). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Archive
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Archive (2020) clearly understood what makes drama work — the same quality that keeps Ex Machina rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Terminator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Terminator (1984) is the kind of film Ex Machina fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The science-fiction overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: 2001: A Space Odyssey shares Ex Machina's gift for science-fiction — the 1968 film delivers mystery without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Dead Poets Society
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Ex Machina compelling — the drama, the atmosphere — shows up in Dead Poets Society (1989) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Minority Report
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction energy of Ex Machina is alive in Minority Report (2002), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Twelve Monkeys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Twelve Monkeys (1995) operates in the same science-fiction as Ex Machina, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Chappie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Chappie (2015) belongs to the same conversation as Ex Machina — both anchor their science-fiction in crime emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ex Machina?
faq.answer: Try Her, Blade Runner 2049, A.I. Artificial Intelligence—each shares DNA with Ex Machina while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ex Machina?
faq.answer: Ex Machina (2015) is best described as Drama, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ex Machina worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
